data.description:
## Symptoms (hit live 2026-06-03 during l10n pane orchestration)

1. **Registration race on new-split.** `c11 new-split right` returns `OK surface:N workspace:M`, but an immediate follow-up command targeting `surface:N` (e.g. `c11 default-agent launch --in-surface surface:N`) fails with `Surface not found: surface:N`. Seconds later the surface appears in `c11 tree` and is addressable by `send`/`read-screen`.

2. **default-agent launch resolution stricter/different from send.** Even ~30s after creation, `c11 default-agent launch --in-surface surface:269` kept failing with `Surface not found` while `c11 send --workspace workspace:1 --surface surface:269` and `read-screen` on the same ref succeeded. Surface JSON showed `tty: null` at the time — launch may require a fully-wired PTY, or resolve refs against a different/stale registry than send.

3. Possibly related: an earlier attempt (surface:268) had `default-agent launch` return plain `OK`, yet the agent never booted and the pane was gone from the tree minutes later — no tracks from the prompt it was given.

## Repro sketch
`OUT=$(c11 new-split right); SURF=$(echo $OUT | grep -oE 'surface:[0-9]+'); c11 default-agent launch --in-surface $SURF --cwd /path --prompt-file /tmp/x.md` → Surface not found.

## Workaround
Create split → sleep 3 → verify in tree → `c11 send --workspace W --surface S "claude --dangerously-skip-permissions '<prompt>'"` instead of default-agent launch.

## Suggested fix directions
- Make new-split's reply synchronous with registry insertion (ref addressable when the envelope returns).
- Align default-agent launch's surface resolution with send's; if it needs the PTY, wait/retry internally rather than erroring.
- Audit why launch returned OK on surface:268 without booting anything.
data.title: new-split surface ref not immediately addressable; default-agent launch fails with 'Surface not found'

provenance.reason: Hit live during l10n pane orchestration 2026-06-03

data.body: Correction to symptom 3: the surface:268 launch DID boot and completed its task (shipped PR #224, merged to main). The pane was closed after finishing — so symptom 3 is not a launch failure. The live bugs are symptoms 1+2: ref-not-yet-addressable race after new-split, and default-agent launch failing to resolve a surface that send/read-screen resolve fine.
PR #229: https://github.com/Stage-11-Agentics/c11/pull/229 — new-split refs immediately addressable + default-agent launch resolution aligned with send. All CI green, ready for operator review.

PR #229 open, all CI green (build, compat-tests, drawbridge-review/judge/gate, mailbox-unit, remote-daemon-tests all pass): https://github.com/Stage-11-Agentics/c11/pull/229

Root cause (both symptoms 1+2): default-agent launch's ref resolution diverged from send.

- CLI: launch resolved --in-surface surface:N via surface.list with EMPTY params (focused workspace only), so a ref in any other workspace failed 'Surface not found' client-side before reaching the app — while send, given an explicit --workspace, resolved fine. Fix: resolve the ref/index across every workspace in every window (surface:N is unique within a workspace), honoring an explicit --workspace/CMUX_WORKSPACE_ID scope. New --workspace flag added + documented.
- Server: launchInExistingSurface resolved via tab.terminalPanel(for:) without v2RefreshKnownRefs() and sent into a possibly-unattached PTY (tty: null). Fix: run v2RefreshKnownRefs() first (parity with send's Phase A) so a just-minted surface:N handle is in the map, and kick requestBackgroundSurfaceStartIfNeeded() when the ghostty surface hasn't attached so the shell boots; sendSubmitFormText queues the line for flush-on-attach, making the OK truthful. Stays on main actor via reentrant v2MainSync (no DispatchQueue.main.sync on a telemetry path).

Tests: extracted pure launch-line composition into DefaultAgentLaunchComposition.plan(...) with 11 runtime tests in c11LogicTests (mirrors CwdParamResolutionTests); full c11-logic suite green. The socket-level attach/registration TIMING is only live-verifiable and is noted as such rather than faked.

Symptom 3 was a non-issue per the ticket comment (surface:268 booted + shipped PR #224). Not merging — stopping at review per instructions.
